reviews.review_description: I like the idea, but when I set it up the image appeared in front of my treadmill. I tried to move it back into the space that I occupied but failed. More to the point when I emailed the developers weeks ago I heard nothing.￼

developer_response.body:
Hi mountaindoc,

Our Support Team reviewed our emails from the past few weeks and was unable to find the message you referenced. Please email us at support@octonicvr.com, and our team will be happy to troubleshoot the issue and help get you up and running.

Sincerely,
The Octonic Team

reviews.review_description: This is an amazing idea. But, I couldn’t get past the treadmill type selection screen. My room is well lit. No sunlight. Open area around me. And it didn’t matter if I was using hand tracking or the controllers. Couldn’t select anything. Occasionally, when randomly clicking and pointing I was able to select something but not at all what I was trying to. I made sure I didn’t run into any of the support issues they listed and still couldn’t get it to work properly. I gave up because it’s a bad sign if it’s that hard to get started. Other apps with similar tech work way better. I’ll try again someday if they make updates. I was on a Meta Quest 3 using a BOBOVR S3 Pro strap.

developer_response.body:
Hi, Chi3farious,

We tested the app multiple times over several days and were not able to replicate the issues you described. If you got stuck on the first screen when launching the app, please check:

When using hand tracking, please make sure that you are physically pressing the buttons in our app with your index finger like you would do on a real button in order to interact with them.

When using the controllers, please make sure that both are active and that you can see a blue beam coming out of one of the controllers. You can use the blue beam to aim at what you would like to interact with and select it using the trigger button of that controller.

If you passed the first screens but got stuck at the “Treadmill Connection” step, please email support@octonicvr.com and we will help.

reviews.review_description: Crashed 4x just trying to open the app. Once the app finally opened, it kept putting the menu above my head. Trying to move the menu down to eye level caused it to “get stuck” to the controller and and had to restart for a 5th time. Finally get into the app on the 6th try only to have obnoxious repetitive music forced on you until you get everything calibrated. Once able to get into the settings, turn the music slider down, but did the music turn down? Nope. It turned off the voiceover (or whatever name they called it) setting. Changing the voiceover setting also controls the voiceover setting. So no way to turn the music off. Have to create an account to view trails, no “freebie” trails whatsoever, have to buy even their trail that looks like early 2k low poly trash, and have to create an account just for demos.

reviews.review_title: It’s not clear how to use it
reviews.review_description: I press start, but everything just stands still; it doesn't even prompt me to connect to the treadmill in the trial mode. Do I really need to buy the app just to see how it works?

developer_response.body:
Hi Victor1237,

Can you please share the mode you were using? If you are Connected Mode, you'll be prompted to connect to the treadmill before proceeding in the app.

Feel free to reach us at support@octonicvr.com to speak with our team about this.

Sincerely,
The Octonic Team

reviews.review_description:
Octonic is an outstanding app that combines fitness and virtual reality in an innovative and engaging way.
Being able to train on the treadmill while exploring virtual environments makes the workout unique and far more motivating than traditional exercise.

The interface is intuitive, the movements are smooth and realistic, and the app provides a strong sense of immersion. Another great strength is the team’s dedication to continuous updates and improvements, which clearly shows their passion and attention to detail.

I highly recommend Octonic to anyone who wants to transform their workout into an exciting and enjoyable journey. Congratulations to the team for making possible something that, not long ago, seemed like science fiction!
I have tried and tried to like this ,  but I can’t ,  using hand tracking it difficult and the console keeps moving without me touching it .     Very annoying app.

developer_response.body:
Hi KittyKatzz,

We believe that you may be experiencing a tracking issue. We recommend using Octonic in a well-lit area so that your headset can properly track your movement. You may have a better experience using the controllers. Our platform allows to use both controllers and hand tracking interchangeably, so all you'll need to do is pick up your controllers to navigate our interface.

If you have any other questions, please reach out to us at support@octonicvr.com. Our team is happy to provide additional assistance.

Sincerely,
The Octonic Team

reviews.review_description: Not worth it if your treadmill not able to connect to this app

developer_response.body:
Hi GoldSphynx6818,

We have a list of connected treadmills on our website: https://octonicvr.com/connectedtreadmills. If you see your treadmill on this list and are unable to connect, please reach out to us directly at support@octonicvr.com for assistance.

If your treadmill isn't listed, you're still able to use Octonic in Non-Connected Mode. Many of our users who use this mode enjoy their experience.

Please let us know if you have any other questions.

Sincerely,
The Octonic Team

reviews.review_description: Nowhere in the description dose it mention you need to make an account or sign up. The very short trial level was so low poly and saying boring is an understatement. The only nice thing I can say about this app is the speed buttons worked. You could probably make your own in unity in less than a day.
Not intuitive AT ALL. Need a immediate exit button extension!

My experience with the trial was positive, but creating an account proved frustrating. The registration process failed, and the initial password reset link was completely unresponsive. After contacting support, they eventually provided a working password reset link, which was helpful. However, it's a shame this entire process was so unnecessarily difficult. On top of that, the app itself has crashed quite a lot, further impacting the overall experience.

Before Octonic my treadmill was so boring but now I eagerly start my daily workout. Love it. It was very difficult to find this app. If you search for treadmill in Meta store you don't find it. On facebook one lady mentioned it and wow! Also, now it's perfect, so please don't change anything. Just build new worlds. Thx 🙏

I have a treadmill and thought, why not give it a try?  Many of the reviews mentioned bugs, I thought, surely they won't be too bad.  What makes them particularly inexcusable is the straightforward nature of the app.  You go to a menu, you select a spot, you walk.  The state is pretty known, it's not like a complex PVP game or something of that nature.  In the first 5 minutes of using it I found a) pointer disappearing, b) buttons not responding within the environment, so no speed/stop start controls operating, c) visual glitches like a kangaroo hopping around even though I've set it to hide the environment.  

The environments themselves are so boring and low poly, I just can't picture anyone feeling engaged while trudging around them.  Set something similar up on youtube, or watch a show.  It's free to try, so no harm giving it a go if you're desperate.

developer_response.body:
Hi glinkot,

Thank you for trying Octonic and providing a detailed account of your experience. If you are having issues interacting with buttons, you can try switching between hand and controller tracking methods. In hand tracking mode, only your index finger can interact with buttons. For treadmills specifically, the speed controls are disabled until you reach 2 mph for safety reasons, as many treadmills start at that speed. Our hide environment button is behaving as intended and only hides the background environment depending on which world you are in.

We hope this helps, and if you run into any other issues or have questions, please email us at support@octonicvr.com

Sincerely,
The Octonic Team

reviews.review_description: I download the winter lake map, click launch and nothing happens so I thought I'd reload the game and try and it tells me to download the map again so I did and again nothing happens when I click launch. So I uninstalled and reinstalled and the same thing happend. Quest 3. Would have paid for all maps but no point if It won't even let me try the included map. The free demo map worked though.

developer_response.body:
Hi SUP3RMASS1VE,

Thank you for trying Octonic. We have launched an update that should resolve any issues you have with downloading additional worlds. Please ensure you are on the latest version of Octonic and try downloading the map again. 

We hope this helps, and if you run into any other issues or have questions, please email us at support@octonicvr.com

Sincerely,
The Octonic Team

reviews.review_description:
I just recently bought a Meta Quest 3 VR headset for exercise.  I always found it boring on my own treadmill but now with the Octonic App time fly’s.
As a 67 year old it was quite easy to setup even though my treadmill was an older type, I do have the odd issue where the controller seems to swop from the left to the right for no apparent reason but it’s not a big problem for me.  If I had to change one thing it would be to incorporate the music from my own collection on my phone.
I rate this app very highly as it has transformed my treadmill  experience.😀👍👍👍

Thank you for making this game compatible with stationary bikes! I truly enjoy the rides and appreciate the variety. This is exactly what I was looking for to make my workouts more fun and motivating. My only request to the developers is to add more stationary bike-compatible rides—even if they come at an additional cost—because I absolutely love the ones currently available. I also use the Holofit app, which offers similar bike rides, but the movement around curves isn’t very smooth and sometimes gives me motion sickness. Your rides, however, are absolutely perfect. Please keep developing more!

I’m 75 and try my best to stay active and fit.  Just Retired from my second career and wanted to add my treadmill back to daily routine. BORING! But I saw this app and decided to give it try. So after my morning workout routine I set up the VR treadmill app on my  quest 3. (Yes I’m a techie from previous occupation). Well I tried the fantasy walk first.  Well indeed it reminded me of “The Way of Water” Avatar with some of the scenes. But much more cartoonish.  Needless to say it had its interesting aspects. I found I wanted to make it all the way to the end of the loop. This doubled my workout time to 45 minutes. Ya I’m old and rusty. But I keep at it. Anyway I look forward to exploring all the rest.  I only hope that updates and places keep coming. Look forward now to my workouts.

developer_response.body:
Hi JingleBob6410,

Thanks for your review! We're excited to hear that you're enjoying Octonic as part of your exercise routine. If you have any questions or specific feedback, feel free to reach out to us at support@octonicvr.com.

Sincerely,
The Octonic Team

reviews.review_description: I really wanted to like this game to enjoy my time on the treadmill more. But it has so many glitches and it's very irritating.

developer_response.body:
Hi Corginut,

Thank you for trying Octonic! Can you email us at support@octonicvr.com with more details of the glitches that you are experiencing? We would be glad to help you resolve these issues. 

Sincerely,
The Octonic Team

reviews.review_description:
It's a half baked app with more bugs than letters in the alphabet but what works works decent . 
Unfortunately they dont seem to update it often. 
To recap, the app has the potential to be a kick arsh training companion.

developer_response.body:
Hello NikosKont,

Thanks for your review. We're excited to hear your thoughts about Octonic's training capabilities. We are always open to hearing feedback, so if you see any bugs please reach out to our team at support@octonicvr.com and we will get to work on them.

Sincerely,
The Octonic Team

reviews.review_description: I have been doing the demo seems pretty cool just wondering how much is it and is it a one time fee or what?

developer_response.body:
Hi,

Thank you for trying Octonic! Our worlds are one-time, individual in-app purchases for lifetime access.

Sincerely,
The Octonic Team

reviews.review_description:
So I've been looking for a treadmill/ cross trainer app for ages... Most are only usable if they are connected via Bluetooth or are not really user-friendly because of the direction change with the controllers or headsets or indeed are just poorly executed !

However this is a game changer, I will be putting some YouTube content up on this app soon. I'll put a link to my channel below, but you really can use this app on a non-connected treadmill, cross trainer, or exercise bike to burn those calories...

It's not perfect there's not nearly enough content and the free stuff's limited to distance the subscription is pricey but it's the best out there in my opinion of the ones I've tried... 

One of the best features is that you can have AI running partners and those partners can be tailored to run with you to encourage you or can be a beginner level so they're running behind you or indeed an elite level so that they are striving ahead to give you someone to aim to catch up with...

Another good feature is with the touch of a button within the interface you can go from immersive to pass through if you need to which is great as well....

Well done Devs on a great app, well executed, great interface if just a wee bit fiddly with hand controls... I'll be investing in the full paid app and hope you will bring more maps and more content soon... 


New channel 😀 
https://youtube.com/@liveonlifereviews?feature=shared
Waste of time, the app doesn’t work as expected

developer_response.body:
Hi Jkbytes,

Can you please email us at support@octonicvr.com with more information about what you are experiencing? We would be glad to help resolve any issues for you.

Sincerely, The Octonic Team

reviews.review_description: I bought a scene and had problems with it the first time but now it's working perfectly. I also tried it on an elliptical on manual speed. It's more fun to see the beautiful scenery while doing it.
I only see 8 different environments to walk/run in. This is a great concept but for $60 I need much more variety.

reviews.review_title: Very good start for an excellent app
reviews.review_description: This is a very good start for what is going to be an excellent exercise app.  I love the pass through and the ability to see the treadmill.  This makes it super comfortable.  The interface is good.  The scenery is a bit boring.  It needs more animals, plants, bushes, etc.  The music selection is ok.  Please add the ability to play youtube music.  Also, as another commenter mentioned, the pace seems to be off.  I am using a non-bluetoothed treadmill.   When I set my treadmill to 3.5 mph and the app to 3.5 mph my feet are going faster than the scenery moves.  It does keep perfect time with the steps of the avitar though.  Anyway, to get it to feel right with the movement of the scenery, I consistently have to set the app to go 1mph faster than my treadmill.  Otherwise it just feels off.  I know you've responded that the pace is accurate - but it doesn't look and FEEL accurate.  Can't wait to see more worlds.

developer_response.body:
Hi JulieMay64,

We’re happy to hear you’re enjoying the app so far.

We’ve noted your feedback for additional details in our running worlds and have recently added more dynamic features to them, including additional wildlife in the Sky Island and Caves and Canyons worlds. As we move forward, we will continue to focus on this.

We also recently expanded our music library and will continue to evaluate additional updates. It’s good to hear you found the new offering compelling. Currently, playing music from other sources such as Youtube is against the terms of use of the platforms. But we’ll keep trying and will be reaching out to these music platforms requesting licensing.

As for scenery speed vs real world speed, thank you for a detailed explanation of what you’re experiencing on your end. This is helpful. Can we ask you to do just one experiment: go to Caves and Canyons and teleport using the map to the Smooth Rocks area. Try running there and tell us if you still feel disconnected in speed. If you don’t this would mean your issue relates to frames of reference: objects may be at a distance from runners and as a result will move slower than objects closer to them. In our more recently released worlds like Sky Island and Spaceminer, we’ve worked on placing objects closer to the running path to mitigate perceived differences in running speeds and will continue to do so in all our upcoming worlds. If however, after trying Smooth Rocks you still feel the speeds are not aligned, please let us know via email below. 

Again thank you for your constructive feedback. Please reach out to us at support@octonicvr.com with the feedback on your test.

Sincerely,
The Octonic Team

reviews.review_description: My treadmill faces a wall and it's boring except a mirror. This program is exactly what I needed. I can walk on a beautiful path and because my treadmill can incline I can go downhill or uphill. Walking like this makes time to go by faster so 30 minutes is nothing but a walk in the park.

I just got a Nordictrack treadmill and wanted a VR option to use while walking.  I found this and it is amazing!  I get so bored just walking to music after a while, but this is an absolute game changer!  I look FORWARD to walking daily and really hate missing a day all due to this app!  Are there a few glitches?  Sure, but the app is still in development, but the pros outweigh any con! I hope the developers continue to expand the world's and add more to them.  Love, love, love this app!

I'm enjoying the app and the developer support is just great!
I see a huge potential for this app. It's already a lot of fun and I believe that with new headsets and a PC version (if possible), we'll be to walk in some awesome places.
I already like it a lot!

developer_response.body:
Hi Eli,

We are happy to hear you’re enjoying Octonic. Our team is already working on developing the app for the new, upcoming generation of headsets, which will be smaller, lighter, and more powerful, allowing us to deliver even more impressive exercising experiences. Keep a lookout!

Sincerely,
The Octonic Team

reviews.review_title: Good way to break up the routine
reviews.review_description: I like the idea of this, I think it’s great when you want scenery when you are walking on a treadmill I would really love it if they could make this where you could watch YouTube videos or you could watch your favourite streaming services with this. I think that would definitely put it over the top. I liked the idea of walking with people and being able to play your music and feel immersed in an environment I wish it looked a bit more realistic but overall I really liked it, it’s a good way to change up the routine. It does get a little scary when it ends you have to stop treadmill before the screen shuts off or you can’t see where your going maybe they could keep that part visible for safety. I only tried the demo I don’t know if it’s different in the full version.

reviews.review_title: I am enjoying this so much!
reviews.review_description:
I am loving this!  Don’t hesitate to purchase this.  I have been wanting for years a VR game/app like Holofit that lets you use the treadmill.  The way it is set up makes it safe as it calibrates to your personal treadmill.  I love the current environments and look forward to new ones coming out.  It simulates being outside and with my treadmill fan on makes it seem even more realistic.  The music is also so positive and motivating.  The time just speeds by when I am using it and makes the often boring treadmill experience so much more fun.  I am so happy you came up with this!   

As it is in the early stages hoping to see some varied environments in the future.  Some suggestions I would like to see besides the current desert scenes might be a beach paradise landscape, a wooded forest, and a downtown town or city scene.

I have an exercise bike with a cadence sensor that I use for other apps. Looking for an alternative, I tried this. Setting up was not very straightforward and I had an issue where the touch screen sat behind my bike’s console so I had difficulty in pressing the controls. It looks like you can move the screen but that control was also behind the console so couldn’t use it. 

I trialled the very short trial I thought that the movement and graphics were great and it would be a useful addition for my fitness. I thought I might as well give the 3 month subscription a go to experience the full app and everything it does. Unfortunately, the bike can only access a very small number of the worlds! I must have missed where this was explained but it was something I did not know prior to paying for 3 months. Had I known this, I would not have signed up. 

I have been offered a refund which is great as I think the app has great potential just not for me at this moment

reviews.review_title: Too bugged to use.  Too bugged to quit.
reviews.review_description:
Wish I could give a negative number of stars.  Constant sputtering/freezing.  Froze during initial setup. 

Developers shouldn't be able to remap the Meta button which makes it impossible to return to the Meta menu screen.  Had to figure out how to do a hard reset of my headset.

developer_response.body:
Hi Brian,

Can you please email us at support@octonicvr.com with more details about your experience? We would like to know what headset you were using, as well as what mode you were on so that we may try to reproduce your experience on our end. Please note that our app is only compatible with Quest 2 and newer devices.

Additionally, we have not remapped the default Meta button functionality as it is not possible. If you run into similar problems in the future, we recommend restarting the headset.

Sincerely,
The Octonic Team

reviews.review_description:
I tried the demo, not sure it gave me a picture of what this app is really like. Couldn’t change the avatar. The scenery was weird, just up in the sky.
I’d thinking about purchasing but want to know for 39.99 is it significantly better?

developer_response.body:
1/19/23 Update: Hi Dya, we wanted to follow up and make sure you were able to follow our recommendations to properly experience our app. Our team is standing by at support@octonicvr.com and ready to help.

Hello Dya, 
 
You don’t need to purchase content in our App to demo the different worlds we offer. On the left control panel (called “Worlds”) just scroll to the right to see and try other worlds. Also to change the Avatar you just need to sign up/log in using the central control panel. After you do it, you will find Avatar settings in the top right corner of the control panel you used to sign up. Again all the above options are available in the App for free. We would encourage you to give Octonic a second shot and believe your opinion will change.If you encounter any problems or still have questions, please reach out to us at support@octonicvr.com. We’re here to help. 
 
Sincerely,
The Octonic Team

reviews.review_description: All I can access is one demo of caverns. There is no option to purchase or see more. I’ve looked while using the vr and on the app, but there is nothing to click on.

developer_response.body:
Hi Rosalie,

Can you please email us at support@octonicvr.com with your account details so we can check for you? After logging into Octonic, you should see a "Get Access" button under the worlds you do not own. 

Since you only see one world, another possibility is that you are on an old version of our application. If that is the case, please uninstall and reinstall the application directly from app lab - Meta auto-update may be failing.

Sincerely,
The Octonic Team

reviews.review_description:
The UI is dreadful, music selection horrible and graphics poor... But it is a distraction when running inside that is not subscription based (ignoring for the moment the limited locations included with the base). 
developers -
If you make me put down controllers to launch your game... dont then expect a bunch of precise inputs... enter a keyboard that is half under my treadmills control panel.   you can insist controlers are put down - perhaps right before running... 
Music - better than when it was on sidequest - but really needs work.   Seems the only tracks are ambient and elevator jazz. 
graphics/movement - its smooth - but I am sure you can get more interesting textures, better shadows... something of interest?  
Extra levels for in-app purchase... Really - only three low-poly basic runs in the base version (one of which was in the side-quest version).  You could have realeased it as free for one level...  I was hoping to pay you and try something new... not struggle to make an account in the above mentions UI, then realize I cannot do any mulitplayer or run against bots... just elevator jazz in mystical land.

developer_response.body:
Hi Westy,
You were probably using the old 1.4 version of the game (the review may have been posted by Meta on Dec 23d with a delay, while the new version was available starting Dec 20th of 2022). Please click "Update" next to Octonic in the Meta store and you’ll have the new version.  
The updated version (2.0 and up) covers most of your comments:
1. Controllers: Controllers input (in addition to hand tracking) is allowed now in all navigation menus of the App. You only need to put down the controllers right before running for your own safety.
2. Music: There are a few different genres you can select from, incl. Rock, Dance, Relaxation, etc. Just go to settings on the front panel in VR home and scroll to Music. You need to be logged in to have access.  
3. Graphics/movement: Please check out our free demo of the Sky Island world. Graphics wise, it's better than anything we've done before. 
4. Extra worlds: Caves and Canyons Sunset full track and Sky Island Demo short track were added compared to SQ demo for free. New worlds added as an In-App purchase in version 2.0 include : SprintBlaster (shooter), Sky World (Full track).  
5. Additionally lots of UI-UX improvements were made that should make the whole experience much smoother.      
If you still have issues, please don't hesitate to contact us at support@octonicvr.com.
Best,
The Octonic Team

reviews.review_description: I just purchased the one they offered for $10. And it's ok but watched some of the other demos and they looked better but no option to buy, only sign up for 3 ,6 or 12 months to use them. I do have to reset my configurations every time I use it as I have an older treadmill. I also use ear buds and listen to my own music and just turn down my oculus. I am interested in more places but will wait a while till more are available as their website shows.

developer_response.body:
Hi Sherry,

We’re happy to hear that you like the app so far.

We offer a few different options for full access to our app. In addition to the 3, 6, and 12-month subscriptions you mention, we also offer ALL running trails a la carte or as an all-worlds package. Seems like you didn’t see the option for individual world purchases for some of the worlds. If this is the case, can you please email us at support@octonicvr.com and specify the names of the worlds you don’t see a purchase option for? 

Using your own phone and headphones to play your favorite music while using Octonic is definitely a great solution. Also we recently added to our music library across all genres in our latest update last week. In order to have access to them, make sure you accept prompts to update the app, if you haven’t done so already. We will continue to grow our library and are also in talks to license popular music. Please follow us on social media to get the most up-to-date announcements.

If you created an account and are logged in to the same account every time you exercise on the treadmill, your configurations will be saved and you should never have to reset them. Please give that a try and let us know if that continues to happen. Just reach out to us at support@octonicvr.com and our team will happily assist.

Sincerely,
The Octonic Team

reviews.review_description:
I REALLY want to love this. My first session was INCREDIBLE so I decided to buy a monthly subscription. The second session was full of glitches. Nothing worked. I couldn’t recalibrate the screen. It was really frustrating. 

I’m pretty upset to have spent $24 for something that doesn’t really work. The speed is way off. My avatar was consistently reporting higher mileage than me.

For reference, Supernatural, another fitness app with INCREDIBLE graphics and music is only 18.99/month..

Once the glitches are fixed and there are better graphics, I’d LOVE to use this. Until then, I’d recommend sticking with the free version. 

It would also be great to use your own music inside or have hits.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your feedback. Please see our answers below. 

Customer Question: I REALLY want to love this. My first session was INCREDIBLE so I decided to buy a monthly subscription. The second session was full of glitches. Nothing worked. I couldn’t recalibrate the screen. It was really frustrating. 
Octonic Answer: We have just tested the App extensively and recalibration worked fine. No other issues were discovered. Please reach out to us at support@octonicvr.com with more details about what went wrong with recalibration (what step, what didn’t work specifically) and we’ll get back to you swiftly. 

Customer Question: I’m pretty upset to have spent $24 for something that doesn’t really work. 
Octonic Answer: Based on the above and below we believe the application is working properly, however, you can get a full refund within a week after buying a 1 month subscription. Unfortunately, App Lab doesn’t allow refunds for In-App purchases (incl. subscription), but no worries: just drop us a line at support@octonicvr.com and we’ll refund right away. All we need is your preferred method of payment such as PayPal or Quickpay.

Customer Question: The speed is way off. 
Octonic Answer: This should not be the case: whatever the speed you set up in VR, that's the speed the VR world will be moving down to 0.1MPH. 1). Please make sure that your physical treadmill and virtual control panel have speeds set to the same units. In your specific session today we see the VR speed set up to miles. This means your physical treadmill should have been set up to miles as well. You can easily change miles to kilometers (if needed) in Octonic’s Settings - Units of measure. 2). If the above is not applicable, but  you still had an impression that the world was moving slower than the treadmill speed, it can be related to the placement of virtual objects you were referencing, which can be quite at a distance from the runner (like the remote rocks). This gives you the perspective that you’re moving slower relative to them. For example, if you run in the Smooth Rocks section of the Caves and Canyons where the rocks are much closer to the runner, you will feel the speed more. 

Customer Question: My avatar was consistently reporting higher mileage than me. Octonic Answer:  1) If you’re referring to avatar legs, they may be not synchronized with your leg movement just because everybody has a different height and related stride length. 
2) If you’re referring to the total mileage difference between VR treadmill and your physical treadmill, we tested  to a less than 1% deviation in total mileage when operated correctly. 

Customer Question: For reference, Supernatural, another fitness app with INCREDIBLE graphics and music is only 18.99/month. 
Octonic Answer: While we agree that SN is a great fitness application it does not offer treadmill running. Because the position of the user is not changing significantly during the exercise, SN can use static 3D visuals (including 360 photos). Octonic offers miles of running with constantly changing  scenery. As a result, we can’t use static 3D visuals, Instead we create a full model of each running world, which requires much more computational power from the mobile CPU (XR2 processor) not allowing for perfect photo realistic visuals.  With that said, we’ve been making constant progress over time: Caves and Canyons was much better than earlier developed Clear Lake, in turn the most recent Sky Island is more visually impressive than Caves and Canyons. We will continue pushing for graphical improvement and are confident that our efforts coupled with hardware improvements down the road will allow closer to photorealistic visuals.

It would also be great to use your own music inside or have hits.  
Octonic Answer: There are a few different genres you can select from, incl. Rock, Dance, Relaxation, etc. Just go to settings on the front panel in VR home and scroll to Music. You need to be logged in to have access. Alternatively, you can always mute the music in the game and listen to your own music with headphones. If you have access to a Quest pro, you will also be able to play Spotify or YouTube music in the background due to its multitasking capabilities. Unfortunately this is not supported on the Quest 2 due to the lack of such functionality. 

Octonic Answer: We hope the above covers most of your concerns. For those issues that are not fully addressed, we really want to spend more time trying to understand what caused the issues on your end. We would like to ask you to please reach out to us at support@octonicvr.com so that we can get more details from you and make Octonic better. 

Best,
The Octonic Team

reviews.review_description: I logged out, and now i cant log in anymore. Can anyone help me pls ?

developer_response.body: Hi RHUMA, happy to help. Please contact us at support@octonicvr.com and a team member will assist and get you up and running.

reviews.review_title: great idea but needs a lot of changes
reviews.review_description:
Couldn't convince me to subscribe. Gives you a few minutes to try but it's still limited (no music, not full experience, etc). The treadmill person also blocked my vision so that's annoying and another deterrence from subscribing (perhaps I could lower the treadmill pace demo doll but it took a lot of front vision so can't miss the treadmill dummy. 

It's boring and fake vr. Too bad there's nothing in real life. Like the streets of Seoul, etc etc. if it had real life videos, that would be enchanting. Otherwise, I don't see any incentive until they make these changes.

NEW: I was disappointed at first, I bought the app and there was no content, but then I saw that I could buy all worlds for around 40bucks, gave it a try and adopted it ;). good work octonic team. I will put 4 stars, the app is great overall I spent 1hout in it the first time I used it. Tha app is missing some "life" I mean, birds, animals, cars,... it just feel a little bit too much empty. But it will improve in the future I'am sure of that. At the end 40bucks for all worlds is a good price.



OLD:I bought the app and there are only demos . I don't have access to anything with the app I paid ? Only demos ?
I will change my rating if you help me find how to enable the full world

developer_response.body:
Hi marcpow,

We’re happy to see you got it working. Our app is a FREE download and access to our full worlds is available through in-app purchases or subscriptions. It’s our goal to make the app easily accessible to allow people to try it before buying it.

Thank you for your comments. We have been working on making the running worlds dynamic with more wildlife and will release those updates very soon. We haven’t released any environments with streets yet where the cars you requested would fit in, but this is something we’re contemplating doing in the future.


Thank you for your feedback, and if you have any other ideas, please reach out to us at support@octonivcr.com.

Sincerely,
The Octonic Team
